title: Father, I Bring Thee Not Myself,
content:
Father, I bring thee not myself, --
    That were the little load;
    I bring thee the imperial heart
    I had not strength to hold.

    The heart I cherished in my own
    Till mine too heavy grew,
    Yet strangest, heavier since it went,
    Is it too large for you?
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Father, I Bring Thee Not Myself,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
